title: Tamil Beetroot Poriyal

ingredients.name: beetroot, peeled and grated
visual_checks: about 2 medium beetroots; wear gloves while grating
ingredients.notes: about 2 medium beetroots; wear gloves while grating

subs.name: carrot, grated — cook for only 5 minutes as it softens faster
2 tbsp desiccated coconut rehydrated in 1 tbsp warm water for 5 minutes

steps.text: Heat oil in a kadai over medium flame. Splutter mustard seeds, add urad dal and chana dal (if using) and fry until golden. Add curry leaves, shallots, and green chilli and sauté for 2 minutes.

Add the grated beetroot and turmeric. Toss to coat in the tempering oil. Sprinkle a pinch of salt and stir-fry on medium heat for 8–10 minutes until the beetroot is cooked through and the raw earthy smell gives way to a sweeter, cooked aroma.

Check salt. Remove from heat and scatter the grated coconut over the top. Toss once and serve — the coconut should be fresh and uncooked against the warm beetroot. Serve as a side dish with rice and sambar.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: beetroot → carrot, grated — cook for only 5 minutes as it softens faster; coconut → 2 tbsp desiccated coconut rehydrated in 1 tbsp warm water for 5 minutes; shallot → 1 small red onion, finely chopped
